sourceMovie.title: Doctor Who
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Doctor Who for the same reason it broke through in 1996: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Geoffrey Sax keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The tv movie and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Star Trek
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doctor Who clicked for you, Star Trek (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Doctor Who?
faq.answer: Try Star Trek, The Flash, The Adam Project—each shares DNA with Doctor Who while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Doctor Who?
faq.answer: Doctor Who (1996) is best described as TV Movie, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Doctor Who worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this tv movie lane.
